checks_results.fact_identified: In 2020, AI struggled to solve basic math problems reliably, but by 2025 it could compete in the International Math Olympiad and achieve a gold medal-level performance.
checks_results.feedback: AI achieved gold medal-level performance at the 2025 International Math Olympiad, marking a significant milestone.

checks_results.fact_identified: AI is already widely used by Singaporeans, including for travel planning where it can recommend destinations and generate personalised itineraries.
checks_results.feedback: AI tools are widely used in Singapore for personalized travel planning and itinerary generation.

checks_results.fact_identified: In Singapore's civil service, AI is used in agency call centres to transcribe conversations in all four official languages and generate summaries so officers can focus on serving the public.
checks_results.feedback: AI transcription and summarization tools are used in Singapore civil service call centres to improve efficiency.

checks_results.fact_identified: AI is being combined with robotics and autonomous machines at Tuas Port and studied for automation at Changi Airport.
checks_results.feedback: Tuas Port uses autonomous vehicles and AI-driven fleet management; Changi Airport deploys autonomous tractors and AI-driven automation.

checks_results.fact_identified: GE Vernova's turbine repair facility in Singapore uses AI to detect anomalies in turbine components and alert human operators for precise diagnosis.
checks_results.feedback: GE Vernova's Singapore facility uses AI-powered automated inspection systems for turbine repair.

checks_results.fact_identified: SMEs like Q&M Dental use AI to analyse dental X-rays to help detect fillings, impacted teeth, and tooth decay more accurately.
checks_results.feedback: Q&M Dental employs AI to analyze dental X-rays for improved diagnosis.

checks_results.fact_identified: In the 1980s, Singapore launched a nationwide computerisation drive that equipped students and workers with computer skills and transformed the economy.
checks_results.feedback: Singapore's 1980s computerisation drive was a key factor in developing IT skills and transforming the economy.

checks_results.fact_identified: When the internet arrived in the 1990s, Singapore successfully adopted it and propelled the nation forward.
checks_results.feedback: Singapore established its first internet connection in 1990 and rapidly adopted internet technologies.

checks_results.fact_identified: At PSA, crane operators were retrained to remotely supervise and manage multiple cranes and vehicles, boosting productivity and improving job quality.
checks_results.feedback: Specific details about PSA crane operator retraining for remote supervision are not publicly available.

checks_results.fact_identified: The SkillsFuture Level-Up Programme provides Singaporeans aged 40 and above with $4,000 in SkillsFuture credits and training allowances of up to $3,000 per month for up to 24 months for full-time study.
checks_results.feedback: The SkillsFuture Level-Up Programme offers substantial credits and allowances to support mid-career training.

checks_results.fact_identified: Vinoth Nanda Kumaran, aged 41 in 2025, transitioned from private hire driver to Business Intelligence Developer after using SkillsFuture credits to pursue a diploma in data engineering and continuing his studies in Business Analytics at SUSS.
checks_results.feedback: Vinoth Nanda Kumaran's career transition story is documented and aligns with SkillsFuture support.
